name: use-scroll-lock
title: useScrollLock
description: Prevent body or element scrolling with reference counting. Multiple locks on the same element stack.

content.text: Options for preventing body or element scrolling.
Ref to the element to lock. Defaults to `document.body` when omitted.
Whether the scroll lock is active.
Effect-on-every-render is intentional: React does not re-fire effects when
target.current mutates while the ref object stays stable.
* Locks scrolling on the target element with shared reference counting and
No dependency array on purpose; see hook-level comment above.

docs.description: Prevent body or element scrolling with reference counting. Multiple locks on the same element stack.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Ref to the element to lock. Defaults to document.body when omitted.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the scroll lock is active.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.

notes.title: Reference counting
notes.content: Multiple useScrollLock calls on the same element increment a shared counter. Scrolling is only restored when all locks are released, preventing double-unlock bugs.

notes.title: Standalone
notes.content: useScrollLock does not require KeyboardProvider. It works independently as a copy-paste utility hook.

examples.name: use-scroll-lock-basic
examples.title: Lock body scroll in a modal

content.text: Background scroll is locked while this overlay is visible.
Scroll down to see content. Opening the overlay locks scroll.
Lock a specific scroll container instead of document.body.
Scrolling inside the panel is blocked while locked; the page itself is untouched.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Ref to the element to lock. Defaults to document.body when omitted.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the scroll lock is active.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.
